recent_calls.summary: The user called to schedule a home warranty service appointment through American Home Shield for heating and cooling system inspection. The agent clarified the nature of the request and asked for a preferred appointment time, but the user decided to check their schedule and call back later to provide the details. The call ended with the user planning to call back to finalize the appointment.

Frank McCoy called to schedule a routine AC inspection under his American Home Shield home warranty. The agent confirmed the details, including the dispatch number and preferred appointment time, and noted Frank's interest in receiving information about home maintenance programs. The call concluded with the agent assuring a follow-up from the team and Frank declining to provide feedback due to his schedule.

The user called to address an issue with not receiving an e-verification email for signature verification. The agent confirmed the user's email address and last name spelling, updated the email address, and assured the user that the e-verification email would be resent. The call ended with the user satisfied and no further assistance needed.

The user called to inquire about the time frame for an appointment under the name Emily Sadler. The agent could not find the appointment and offered to connect the user to a live representative, which the user requested. The call was then successfully transferred to a live agent.

Frank McCoy called to schedule a routine air conditioner checkup under his American Home Shield home warranty. He provided his dispatch number and preferred a Friday after 9:00 a.m. appointment. The agent confirmed the details and noted Frank's tentative interest in maintenance programs, with a follow-up expected from the team.

Joe called back to reschedule a missed AC cleaning service. He requested a standard AC cleaning as a renter at 912 Saravalle Drive, Saint Peters, with a preferred time of anytime Sunday, but the agent informed him there were no available slots on Sunday and asked for alternative times. The phone number was corrected during the call.

The user called to set up an appointment for their air conditioner under their HSA home warranty service plan. The agent explained that the appointment could not be booked directly and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The call ended with a successful transfer to another agent.
The user requested to speak to a real person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user called to clarify a service request that was changed by American Home Shield and mentioned being transferred regarding an HVAC tune-up. The agent attempted to understand the user's current service needs but the call ended abruptly before the user could provide more details.
The user called and requested to speak with Cece. The agent attempted to connect the user and successfully transferred the call to the appropriate person.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the initial agent transferred the call to another agent successfully. The call lasted 13 seconds and ended with a call transfer.

The user called in response to a contact from Budget to schedule an air conditioning checkup under their HSA home warranty. The agent confirmed the nature of the appointment and then transferred the call to a live agent as requested by the user. The call ended with a successful transfer to another agent.

The user, Vern Hellman from Jefferson Franklin Community Action Corporation, called to inquire about the timing of an HVAC service appointment for a client, Tracy Portell. The agent could not find an appointment in the system and offered to transfer the user to a live representative, which the user accepted. The call was successfully transferred to a live agent for further assistance.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the call was successfully transferred to another agent for further assistance.
The user requested to speak with a live representative, and the agent successfully transferred the call to another agent. The call lasted 17 seconds and ended with a call transfer.

The user called to set up an appointment for an air conditioning maintenance checkup under their existing maintenance plan. The agent confirmed the nature of the service and then transferred the call to a live representative for further assistance.
The user called and requested to speak with Amy. The agent attempted to connect the user and successfully transferred the call to the requested contact.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the initial agent transferred the call to another representative. The call lasted 19 seconds and ended with a successful transfer.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the initial agent transferred the call successfully to another agent.

Lauren called to get an update on a previous HVAC repair that was not working. The agent scheduled a follow-up service appointment for Monday afternoon, April 6th, and confirmed the booking with Lauren. The call ended with the user declining to provide feedback.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the call was successfully transferred to another agent for further assistance.
The user requested to speak with a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user called after hours to speak with Dan and was informed that Dan is available during regular business hours. The user provided their name and phone number for a callback and requested that Dan return their call without providing additional details.

Tracey called after hours requesting an urgent callback from CJ regarding a busted pipe emergency. The agent documented the emergency and assured Tracey that the team would prioritize the callback on the next business day. The call ended with no further requests from Tracey.

The user called back to inquire about the appropriate time to call the next morning. The agent informed the user that regular business hours start at 8:00 am and offered further assistance, which the user declined.
The user declined to continue the conversation after the agent requested their service address ZIP code and ended the call politely. No further information or assistance was provided during the call.

The user called to inquire about a plumbing service call regarding a valve issue on their water heater, expressing urgency due to lack of gas and needing service that night. The agent explained that after-hours service is not available but assured the request would be prioritized for follow-up the next business day. The call ended with the user needing immediate assistance but no after-hours service could be provided.
The call was very brief and disconnected abruptly after a short exchange with the agent. The user did not complete any request or provide clear information.

Joseph called to confirm his appointment for evaporator coil cleaning scheduled for Tuesday, April 7th at 10 AM. The agent confirmed the appointment details and offered information about maintenance programs, which Joseph declined. Joseph provided positive feedback, rating the experience a 4 out of 5 and describing it as efficient.

Keith Fudge called Budget Air's after-hours assistant to schedule a wire replacement service for his cooling system based on an estimate. The agent confirmed the service details, address, and callback number, and Keith rated the experience positively. The team will follow up next business day to confirm the appointment.

The user, Quintelle, called but decided to call back during normal business hours to speak with CG, indicating no urgency. The agent offered to take a message or arrange a callback, but the user declined and ended the call politely.

The user called to inquire about the whereabouts of their technician but was unclear and frustrated during the brief conversation. The agent attempted to clarify the issue but the call ended abruptly with the user hanging up.

The user, Charles Zini, called after hours to request an estimate for replacing his air conditioner and furnace due to coolant loss. The agent collected his contact information and service address, confirmed details, and arranged for a team member to call him back to discuss the estimate. Charles declined information about maintenance programs.

The user inquired about the types of equipment Budget sells, specifically asking about Trane equipment. The agent explained that Budget primarily installs brands like Lennox, Goodman, and Empire, and does not typically install Trane systems. The call ended abruptly as the user expressed frustration.

The user, Decarla, called after hours to follow up on an insurance claim related to her air conditioner. She had previously received an estimate and wanted to discuss the final outcome. The agent documented her information and assured her that a representative would call her back on the next business day.

The user, Puline, called to request a callback regarding ongoing issues with her air conditioning that were not resolved despite a recent service visit. The agent confirmed the callback number and informed her that a representative would call back on the next business day since live transfers were not available after 4:00 pm. The call ended with no further requests from the user.

The user, Brenda, called to inquire about the efficiency rating of a system quoted by Jeff and requested a callback to discuss options for higher efficiency models. She also asked about participation in home forgiveness programs through the city of O'Fallon. The agent confirmed the details and arranged for Jeff or a team member to contact Brenda the next business day.
The user requested to speak to a human agent, and the call was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to report a recurring air conditioning issue covered under their American Home Shield home warranty, requesting an emergency service appointment for today. The agent confirmed the details, including the dispatch number and correct service address, and informed the user that Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact them shortly. The user declined information about maintenance programs and gave positive feedback on the call experience.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer successfully.

The user called to report that their scheduled AC service appointment was missed, and no one had contacted them or arrived. The agent acknowledged the issue and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.
The user called and requested to speak to Nick. The agent attempted to transfer the call to Nick and successfully completed the transfer.
The user requested to speak to a real person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The user called customer service and the agent, Sophie, promptly transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The call lasted 13 seconds and ended with a successful transfer.
The user requested to speak to a live person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to another live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user, Catherine Bryan, called to check on her appointment scheduled for today between 1:00 and 2:00 p.m. with technician Rich. The agent confirmed the appointment details and then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance as the technician had not arrived yet.

The agent informed the user about Humana Medicare Advantage plans that offer extra benefits, including a zero dollar monthly plan and a prepaid card for over-the-counter items. The user did not respond during the call, which lasted 41 seconds before the user hung up.

The user called to confirm their scheduled appointment and was informed that the appointment with technician Amory is set for today between 1:00 and 2:00 p.m. The user expressed concern about not hearing from the technician yet, and the agent transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user expressed frustration with an automated system and requested to speak with a live agent. The agent acknowledged the frustration and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.
